response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but the fit for this specific role is weak. His professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus a bachelor thesis project, far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. His career centers on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web platforms. He has no Fixed Income, derivatives, or capital-markets systems experience — RIDE Capital was retail-investment fintech, not investment banking — and his SQL background is MySQL/MongoDB rather than SQL Server or Oracle. Desirable extras (CI/CD, scripting, some Java) do not offset missing all mandatory requirements.
strengths: Solid engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and testing culture
Experience with low-latency, high-throughput systems (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
Broad technical range across backend, web, mobile, and embedded, with strong communication skills (talks, workshops)
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience (~7 months, a decade ago) versus the mandatory 5–10 years on Windows
No Fixed Income, capital-markets, or investment-banking domain experience
No demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MongoDB/MySQL

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering and delivery record
